# Project Angerona (AngeronaSuite) — llms.txt

> ⚠️ THIS IS THE CANONICAL APP. Angerona is a **PySide6 desktop GUI**, package `angerona` under `AngeronaSuite/src/angerona/`, launched by `start-angerona.bat` → `pythonw -m angerona`. It is NOT the legacy Rich-terminal prototype at the `D:\local-security-ai` root (`agent.py`/`ui.py`) — that is a separate, older program; do not edit it for GUI work. Read this file first.

## AGENT DIRECTIVES
- CONTEXT FIRST: read this file + `ANGERONA_CAPABILITIES.md` + `ANGERONA_ARCHITECTURE.md` (repo root) before changing anything. The real app = `AngeronaSuite/src/angerona/`.
- Edits = diffs only; never rewrite whole files; no pleasantries. Local-first (no cloud egress without consent); user-mode only (no unsigned drivers); stdlib + PySide6.
- To stop the app: STOP button (`_full_shutdown`) or `kill-all-angerona.bat` — both hard-kill `pythonw.exe`/`python.exe` (elevated). Closing the window only hides to tray (single-instance guard on loopback :47921).

## STACK / LAUNCH
Python 3.10+ · PySide6 GUI · local Ollama (llama3) triage, cloud fallback (Gemini/OpenRouter). Installed editable: `pip install -e .[windows]`. Entry `python -m angerona` → `app.py:AngeronaApp` builds core services + `gui/main_window.py:MainWindow`. Per-user data dir (`Config.data_dir`); DB `flight-recorder.db`.

## ARCHITECTURE MAP (`src/angerona/`)
- `app.py` — wires Config, EventBus, FlightRecorder(storage), ModuleManager, StatusReporter, MainWindow. Subscribes `storage.record`, `incidents.on_event`, `init_log(db_path)`, and `init_tracker().on_event` at startup.
- `core/` — `module_base.py` (BaseModule), `module_manager.py` (auto-discovers modules), `eventbus.py` (Event/Severity/subscribe/publish/recent), `config.py`, `storage.py`, `singleton.py` (:47921), `commands.py` (console), `selftest.py`, `autostart.py`, `privilege.py`, `status_report.py`, `remediation_log.py` (SQLite audit ledger, `init_log()`/`get_log()`), `attack_tracker.py` (live ATT&CK heat; 86 techniques, 14 tactics; `init_tracker()`/`get_tracker()`; `on_event()` bus subscriber; `snapshot()`).
- `gui/` — `main_window.py` (header buttons: Self-Test, INITIATE SHARK ATTACK, 🌐 WORLD VIEW, 🔥 ATT&CK MAP, 🎯 FORENSICS, SETTINGS, STOP; DashboardCards; Modules|Alerts splitter over Console; StatusStrip; **1000 ms two-tier refresh timer**: fast path every tick for StatusStrip+threat, full refresh every 2 ticks for cards/modules/alerts), `pages.py` (StatCard, DashboardCards, ModulesPanel, AlertsPanel, CommandConsolePanel, **StatusStrip two-line chips**, ModuleInspector, AlertDetailDialog, SharkMonitorDialog, AARDialog, SettingsDialog), `animations.py` (ThreatOverlay + SharkSwimIndicator), `theme.py` (StatusStrip QSS, hover highlight, visible splitter handles), `worldview_page.py` (legacy World View dialog), `flow_window.py` (FlowWindow — 2×3 grid 6-step flowchart: CAPTURE→DETECT→AI TRIAGE→RESPOND→ATTACK→SELF-HARDEN with arrowheads, edge labels, live metrics; `flow_metrics.py` feeds it), `attack_heatmap.py` (AttackHeatmapWindow — 14-column ATT&CK matrix, clickable cells, detail panel, 5 s QTimer refresh).
- `modules/` — drop-in `BaseModule` subclasses, AUTO-DISCOVERED by `module_manager` (scans `angerona.modules`, no manual registration): ai_triage, deception, file_integrity, forensics, network_monitor, packet_sniffer, process_monitor, soar, soar_engine, cloud_escalation, yara_scanner, **posture_hardening**, **memory_timemachine (MTM)**, **api_patch_detector (APID)**, **provenance_graph (PROV)**, **speculative_triage (SPEC)**, **intel_sync (INTL)**, **network_protocol_decoder (NDRD)**, **etw_listener (ETWG)**, **flight_cache (MEMC)**, **ipc_guard (AUTH)**, **frz_heartbeat (FRZ)**, **canary_drill (DRILL)**, **hermetic_packager (HERMETIC)**, **sys_bridge (SYS)**, **etw_realtime_sensor (ETWR)**, **ai_model_integrity (AMIG)**, **compliance_mapper (CMAP)**, **siem_forwarder (SIEM)**, **counter_agentic (CAGT)**, **storage_hygiene (SHYG)**.
  **G2 sensor modules (new):** **sysmon_listener (SYSL)** — Sysmon XML + win32evtlog bridge (EID 1/3/6/8/10/25); **mem_inject_scanner (MINJ)** — VirtualQueryEx RWX scanner (T1055), JIT allowlist, 60s dedup; **ransomware_heuristics (RANS)** — Shannon entropy ≥7.9 + mass-rename detector, user-folder watchdog; **wfp_controller (WFPC)** — iphlpapi GetExtendedTcpTable/UdpTable singleton, IPv4+IPv6 (BL-17 fixed), 5s TTL cache; **amsi_bridge (AMSI)** — AmsiScanBuffer consumer (NOT patching), EICAR health check; **wlan_monitor (WLAN)** — evil-twin detection via netsh SSID/BSSID diff; **arp_watchdog (ARPW)** — ARP cache diff + optional scapy sniffer; **av_telemetry_bridge (AVTB)** — Defender Operational log (EID 1116/1117/5001) + Get-MpThreatDetection PS fallback; **dynamic_resource (DRES)** — HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS escalation under load, NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS cooldown after 60s.
  **G3 hardening modules (new):** **kernel_bridge (KRNL)** — PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutineEx ring buffer via DeviceIoControl to AngeronaSensor.sys (kernel/AngeronaSensor/); **fast_path (FPTH)** — 17-rule deterministic IOC library (Mimikatz/PSExec/shadow-copy/AMSI-bypass etc.) that emits CRITICAL immediately bypassing Ollama triage. NOTE: `AngeronaSuite\modules\` (top-level) is only a README — real modules live in `src/angerona/modules/`.
- `engines/` — core_engine, defense_monitor, edr_logger, sniffer, monitor, watchdog, cloud_fallback, reporter, persistence, rag, unified_edr, self_compiler, ai_telemetry, **mcp_server** (`AngeronaMCPServer` — HTTP+SSE MCP 2024-11-05 server on loopback :47923; 6 read-only tools: `get_recent_alerts`, `get_module_status`, `get_attack_heatmap`, `get_incidents`, `get_remediation_log`, `search_events`; opt-in via `config.mcp_enabled`; started in `app.py:start()` after modules load; stopped in `shutdown()`; no data egress).
- `shark/` — `shark_attack.py` (SharkAttackEngine adversary sim), `aar_report.py` (after-action report).
- `telemetry/` — `sensors.py`, **`worldview.py`** (WorldViewEngine).
- `academy/` — Flight Instructor Mode (AI coaching narration), achievements, profiler.

## MODULE CONTRACT (drop-in)
Subclass `core.module_base.BaseModule`; set `name/description/category/version/enabled_by_default`; implement `run()` (daemon loop, poll `self.stopping`, use `self.sleep()`); call `self.emit(msg, Severity.X, **details)` and `self.set_health(pct, note)`; override `self_test()->(bool,str)`. ModuleManager threads/lifecycle it; health_state maps to ok/degraded/critical/failed/off colours.

## NEW / RECENT FEATURES

## LATEST (v1.4.0 — decoupled resilience ecosystem + detection/intel modules)
- **New detection/intel modules** (all `BaseModule`, auto-discovered, `CODE/NAME/state/health_pct/self_test()`+`register()`):
  - **ETWR** (`etw_realtime_sensor.py`) — real-time process-creation via the Kernel-Process ETW provider (pywintrace); closes the polling blind spot. Requires elevation; degrades to "unavailable" (ETWG/polling still cover) off-Windows / no pywintrace. Complements ETWG (Security-channel 4688).
  - **AMIG** (`ai_model_integrity.py`) — SHA-256 attestation of local Ollama model blobs vs a trust-on-first-use baseline (+ content-address self-check); CRITICAL on tamper/poisoning (T1565.001). Read-only, chunked hashing.
  - **CMAP** (`compliance_mapper.py`) — maps live MITRE detections (bus events carrying a technique id) to NIST SP 800-53 + DoD STIG; writes `diagnostics/compliance_report.json`. Sub-technique→base-technique fallback.
  - **SIEM** (`siem_forwarder.py`) — CEF-over-Syslog (UDP/TCP) forwarding; opt-in, idle until `ANGERONA_SIEM_HOST` set (never blasts a default IP). `enabled_by_default=False`.
  - **CAGT** (`counter_agentic.py`) — **defensive detection only**: inference-cadence fingerprinting (~1.5–4 s LLM "think" rhythm) + discovery→action chain + local inference-port (11434) watch. Offensive subsystems from the spec (weaponized prompt-injection, EDoS, adversarial corruption) are intentionally NOT built. Alerts only; mitigation stays with operator-gated SOAR.
  - **SHYG** (`storage_hygiene.py`) — keeps data off C:. Detects Angerona spill at the default `LOCALAPPDATA\Angerona` while `ANGERONA_DATA` points elsewhere; opt-in auto-migrate (`ANGERONA_STORAGE_AUTOMIGRATE=1`), operator-gated `purge_stray(confirm=True)`.
- **INTL Ring 2 — Threat-Intel Fusion** — `intel_sync.py` gained an opt-in STIX/TAXII (or simple JSON) IOC cache with O(1) `is_ip_flagged()` / `is_hash_flagged()` for the network/process sensors; zero network I/O unless `ANGERONA_IOC_FEED` is set.
- **Fix** — `canary_drill.py` imported non-existent module-level `subscribe`/`publish`; rewired to the bound-bus API (`self._bus.subscribe` / `self._bus.publish`). Package now discovers 55 modules, 0 import errors.
- **Decoupled resilience ecosystem** (`src/angerona/resilience/`, opt-in via `ANGERONA_RESILIENCE=1` in `core/headless.py`) — standalone Telemetry Scanner + supervisor keep each other, the core, and the BlackBox alive, feeding RAW telemetry to the core:
  - `heartbeat.py` — AWDG 32-byte mmap heartbeat; frozen-tick + live-pid ⇒ **suspended** (anti-SIGSTOP). `ipc_ring.py` — bounded `ARNG` shared-memory ring (scanner→core), overwrite-oldest + backpressure. `shutdown_token.py` — HMAC (bus.key) nonce stand-down so maintenance halts respawns. `supervisor.py` — detached-process spawn (no shared tree), 0%-idle blocking death detection, respawn with N-fail backoff→SAFE_MODE. `diagnostics.py` — atomic status/thread_dump/tracemalloc/selftest_failures for the read-only BlackBox. `scanner.py` — `python -m angerona.resilience.scanner`. `manager.py` — `start_resilience(bus)` (core-side driver). `selftest.py` — ping loop-integrity + dry-run resurrection.
  - Wire contract in `shared-ipc/CONTRACT.md` (byte layouts) so compiled sensors/watchdog stay byte-compatible.
- **Go hypervisor** (`frz/hypervisor/`, build on Windows via `build.bat`) — multi-way keep-alive (revives core + scanner), honours the same AWDG heartbeat + HMAC stand-down token (interop math verified against Python). Auto-detected by `ResilienceManager`. Honest naming (no process-ghosting).
- **Advanced Management Console** (`gui/upgrade_console.py`, launched from `main_window` CONSOLE button) — Mobile Integration (settings/test to `.env`), AI Sandbox (API keys, Ollama model check, Implement-Code), and Watchdog/Telemetry hubs bound to the LIVE ecosystem heartbeats + `status_*.json`.
- **Bootstrapper** (`Install-Angerona.bat`) — idempotent one-shot: UAC, winget Python 3.10, venv+deps, Ollama + `llama3:8b`, compiles the watchdog, Desktop shortcuts.

- **MCP Server (`engines/mcp_server.py`)** — opt-in `AngeronaMCPServer` (HTTP+SSE, stdlib only, no new deps). Implements MCP 2024-11-05 protocol on loopback `127.0.0.1:47923`. Six read-only tools: `get_recent_alerts` (EventBus ring), `get_module_status` (all module health), `get_attack_heatmap` (ATT&CK tracker snapshot), `get_incidents` (correlator), `get_remediation_log` (audit ledger), `search_events` (FlightRecorder + bus fallback). Config: `mcp_enabled` (bool, default False) + `mcp_port` (int, default 47923) in `Config` and `settings.json`. Toggle in Settings ▸ MCP Server. Connect Claude Desktop: add `"angerona": { "url": "http://127.0.0.1:47923/sse" }` to `~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json`. Started after `manager.discover()` in `app.py:start()`; stopped before `manager.stop_all()` in `shutdown()`. Any bind failure silently disables it without affecting startup.
- **Live MITRE ATT&CK Heatmap (`core/attack_tracker.py` + `gui/attack_heatmap.py`)** — singleton `AttackTracker` subscribed to every EventBus event. Tags are extracted three ways: explicit `mitre_tags` attrs, `_ETW_TAG_MAP` (38 kind→TID mappings), `_PROC_MAP` (22 process-name→TID inferences). `TechniqueHeat` stores hit counts with 24h log-weighted decay (heat 0.0–1.0). 86 techniques across 14 tactics pre-populated so the matrix is always full. `AttackHeatmapWindow` (non-modal QDialog): 14 tactic columns with colour-coded headers, clickable cells on dark→blue→amber→red ramp, 3px heat bar, 5s QTimer refresh, detail panel (TID, count, heat, last event IDs), "Reset counts". `🔥 ATT&CK MAP` button in main_window header. Wired in `app.py` via `init_tracker().on_event` (same singleton pattern as `remediation_log`).
- **World View flowchart redesign (`gui/flow_window.py` + `core/flow_metrics.py`)** — rebuilt from 7-node single-row (1460 px, `fitInView` scale 0.68 → invisible text) to compact 2×3 grid (~780 px, scale ~0.9). Six steps: ① CAPTURE · ② DETECT · ③ AI TRIAGE · ④ RESPOND · ⑤ ATTACK · ⑥ SELF-HARDEN → loop. Each node: colour-coded header band (blue=defensive, purple=AI, red=adversarial, green=self-hardening), 12pt bold white title, 8pt subtitle, live metric badge. Edges: bezier cubic with filled-triangle arrowheads clipped to node boundary and edge-label text (events / alerts / score / gate / weakness / fixed ↺). `flow_metrics.py` supplies per-node live data keyed to the six step IDs.
- **Remediation audit log (`core/remediation_log.py`)** — persistent SQLite table in `flight-recorder.db`; every `apply_remediation()` call writes timestamp, caller, MITRE TID, action key/title, outcome (applied/skipped/dry_run/rolled_back/error), verify result, host-level flag, full return JSON. Console: `remlog [n]` / `remlog <T####>`.
- **Phase 3 hardening modules (4) + performance layer** — `src/angerona/modules/` + `src/angerona/core/` + `src/angerona/gui/`:
  - **FRZ** (`frz_heartbeat.py`) — writes a nanosecond heartbeat to a shared `mmap` file every 500 ms; launches the compiled Go watchdog (`frz/frz_watchdog.go` → `frz_watchdog.exe`) which independently monitors the Python PID and, if the clock freezes >2 s while the process is alive (thread-suspension attack), triggers emergency network isolation + hard-kills the interpreter. Build: `cd AngeronaSuite/frz && build.bat`. Requires Go + `golang.org/x/sys/windows`.
  - **DRILL** (`canary_drill.py`) — fires a synthetic benign `cmd /c REM <UUID>` subprocess every 60 s and listens for the corresponding ETWG 4688 echo on the EventBus within 6 s. Consecutive misses → CRITICAL "telemetry blinding" alert. Detects `ntdll!EtwEventWrite` hooks and Security-channel suppression.
  - **HERMETIC** (`hermetic_packager.py`) — tracks whether Angerona is running as the signed PyOxidizer monolithic binary (`hermetic/pyoxidizer.bzl`, `hermetic/build-hermetic.bat`). Reports health: 100% hermetic+signed / 80% hermetic+unsigned / 55% binary present but running as .py / 30% no binary. `trigger_build()` opens the build terminal — review-gated. Sign output with `signtool`.
  - **SYS** (`sys_bridge.py`) — wraps `syscall_bridge.pyd` (C extension: `syscall_bridge/syscall_bridge.c` + `setup.py`) to route `terminate()`, `suspend()`, `resume()` via **indirect NT syscalls** (SSN read from on-disk ntdll, jump to `syscall;ret` gadget in live ntdll past the function hook site). Falls back to ctypes/psutil if .pyd not compiled. Build: `cd syscall_bridge && python setup.py build_ext --inplace && copy *.pyd ..\src\angerona\modules\`. `get_bridge()` factory for SOAR/posture_hardening callers.
  - **GPU Entropy Pipeline** (`core/gpu_entropy.py`) — `EntropyWorker` daemon thread batches strings (up to 4096 per pass) and computes Shannon entropy via PyTorch CUDA tensors (`get_pipeline().submit_batch()`). CPU→VRAM on uint8 tensor, vectorised one-hot frequency count + log2 entropy on GPU, VRAM→CPU float list back. Falls back to NumPy vectorised then pure-Python. NDRD and Packet Sniffer call `get_pipeline().submit_batch()` instead of looping.
  - **Async GUI Architecture** (`gui/telemetry_worker.py`) — `TelemetryWorker` (QThread) polls MEMC / FlightRecorder and emits `batch_ready` Qt signal; `UIBatchFlusher` (QTimer 100 ms) renders batches in the main thread without per-event repaints; `ConsoleQueryWorker` (transient QThread per SQL command) keeps console input fluid during threat-hunting queries. See `INTEGRATION NOTES` at bottom of the file.
  - **GUI performance + UX (pages.py + main_window.py + theme.py)** — StatusStrip now shows `CODE\npct` two-line chips (QLabel, fixed height 52 px) using `_short_code()` (`.CODE` attr for Phase-2c/2d/3 modules, `_FALLBACK_CODES` for legacy, initials fallback); change-detection dict `_prev` skips stylesheet regeneration for unchanged chips. `ModulesPanel.refresh()` uses `setUpdatesEnabled(False/True)` + per-row skip-unchanged guard. `AlertsPanel` incremental: only new events (ts > `_newest_ts`) prepended; full rebuild only on initial load or gap; `setUpdatesEnabled` batching. `AlertsPanel` uses `QHeaderView.Interactive` (not `ResizeToContents`) for the Module column — O(1) per tick vs O(n). `ModulesPanel._on_click` guards `col == 0` (checkbox) to prevent spurious inspector opens. Timer split: 1000 ms tick; StatusStrip+threat check every tick, full refresh (cards/modules/alerts) every 2nd tick. `theme.py`: `#StatusStrip` QSS rule, `QTableWidget::item:hover` highlight, `QSplitter::handle` 3 px visible grab bars, table item padding 4 px 6 px.
- **Platform extension modules (4)** — same dual `BaseModule`+`register()`/`CODE/NAME/state/health_pct/self_test()` contract, in `src/angerona/modules/`:
  - **NDRD** (`network_protocol_decoder.py`) — decodes DNS query names and scores per-label Shannon entropy / length / digit-ratio to flag DGA + DNS-tunneling; consumes DNS events off the bus. Read-only.
  - **ETWG** (`etw_listener.py`) — captures process-creation (EID 4688) + logon (4624/4672) from the Windows Security channel via `win32evtlog`, republishes to the bus; degrades to psutil process-diff if the channel/audit is unavailable.
  - **MEMC** (`flight_cache.py`) — bounded `sqlite3 :memory:` mirror of the flight recorder (warm from disk + live bus append, LRU-evict at CAP); `recent()`/`query()` are SELECT-only, low-I/O reads. Ephemeral by design.
  - **AUTH** (`ipc_guard.py`) — zero-trust HMAC-SHA256 challenge/response on loopback `127.0.0.1:65432` (default-deny; per-install `os.urandom` key under data_dir; logs spoof attempts). `sign()/verify()/authenticate()` helpers for peers.
  - **INTL verification pass** — `intel_sync.confirm(cve, run_verification=True)` now, after explicit operator approval, tasks the Judgment verifier (`python -m angerona.shark.verify <tid> --verify`; the spec's `shark_attack_gui.py --verify` maps to this) with one mock footprint test and only promotes the detection rule to active if it returns BLOCKED. Still never applies a host fix. — all `BaseModule` subclasses that ALSO expose the `CODE/NAME/state/health_pct/self_test()`+`register()` contract:
  - **MTM** (`memory_timemachine.py`) — lock-light SPSC `mmap` ring (`telemetry_ringbuffer.mmap`) + per-PID sliding hash cache; `delta_for(pid, strings)` forwards ONLY new strings to the Ollama queue (>80% token cut). Strings sourced from psutil-accessible surfaces (no raw RPM).
  - **APID** (`api_patch_detector.py`) — reads pristine `ntdll.dll`/`kernel32.dll` from `System32`, parses export prologues (stdlib PE parser), compares vs live memory (GetModuleHandle→GetProcAddress→`string_at`); flags `E9`/`FF 25`/`68…C3` inline hooks → CRITICAL to `shared_logs/soar_events.json`. Read-only; never hooks/unhooks.
  - **PROV** (`provenance_graph.py`) — builds a typed PROC/FIM/NET DAG from `flight-recorder.db` + live EventBus; `ancestry(pid)`, `subtree(pid)`, `blast_radius(pid)` feed the Blast Radius panel.
  - **SPEC** (`speculative_triage.py`) — subscribes to early markers (unknown proc from temp/AppData/Downloads), pre-streams a snapshot to Ollama (`keep_alive`) so the Step-3 verdict has no cold start; `get_primed(pid)` reuses the warm frame. Offline-safe.
  - **INTL** (`intel_sync.py`) — when online, GETs the CISA KEV catalog (inbound only, no host data egress), correlates vs OS + running services, writes `shared_logs/upstream_threats.json` with remediation + MITRE mapping. Alerts the dashboard; `confirm(cve)` is a review-gated operator hook — NEVER auto-applies fixes.
- **Theme system overhaul (`gui/theme.py`)** — `THEMES` dict with per-theme `alt_row`/`chip_h` keys; `build_qss()` reads these via `setdefault()` fallback. Three themes: `cyber` (default), `crt` (green phosphor), `slate` (neutral blue-grey). New QSS rules: `QTableWidget::item:alternate` alternating row colours, `QHeaderView::section` uppercase with bottom accent border, `QScrollBar:horizontal`, `QToolTip`. `MONO_FONT_FAMILY = "Fira Code"` exported for consistent monospace rendering across all console/code panels. All `QFont("Consolas", 10)` replaced with `QFont("Fira Code", 10)` across `pages.py`.
- **Threat Intel Dashboard (`gui/threat_intel_page.py`)** — `ThreatIntelDashboard(QDialog)`: non-modal, reads `shared_logs/upstream_threats.json` (written by INTL module), 8-column KEV table with alternating rows, ransomware-campaign flag colouring (#ef4444 red), due-date amber/red colouring (≤14d/overdue), 60s auto-refresh. `Stage for Review` per-row button opens QMessageBox with full remediation text — review-gated, never auto-applies. `🔍 Deep Analysis` button (purple, #4c1d95) calls `_open_deep_analysis()` → creates/raises `CveAnalysisWindow`. `self._cve_analysis_dlg: CveAnalysisWindow | None` singleton pattern. `🛡 THREAT INTEL` pulsing button added to main_window.py header; `_update_threat_intel_pulse()` checks INTL `alert_pending` and alternates red/amber style each 1s tick (2s full cycle, zero extra timers).
- **CVE Deep Analysis Window (`gui/cve_analysis_window.py`)** — `CveAnalysisWindow(QDialog)`: two-panel non-modal dialog. Left: `QScrollArea` with `CveCard(QFrame)` widgets, sorted ransomware-first→driver→rest. Right: `QPlainTextEdit` AI fix pane + `⚡ Generate AI Analysis` button. `CveCard`: NVD hyperlink, DRIVER/KERNEL badge (keyword scan of product/vendor/name/remediation for driver/kernel/.sys), RANSOMWARE badge, detail grid (Date Added, Due Date, Required Remediation, Matched Token), CISA action text. `_run_ai_analysis()` spawns daemon thread `CVE-AI-analysis`; `_ai_done = Signal(str)` for GUI-thread delivery (never touches widgets from background thread). Ollama: `127.0.0.1:11434/api/chat`, 60s timeout, llama3 model; `_fallback_analysis()` structured CISA text when Ollama unavailable. AI system prompt: senior Windows security engineer role, ransomware CVEs first, driver-specific kernel mitigations, plain English prioritised list. `showEvent()` reloads CVEs on each open. No host data egress — reads only local `upstream_threats.json`.
- **intel_sync non-blocking fetch (`modules/intel_sync.py`)** — `run()` offloads urllib GET to daemon thread `INTL-fetch` via `threading.Event`; main loop waits in cancellable 1s slices (`_done.wait(timeout=1.0)`) so `self.stopping` is respected during the 30s urllib timeout. 45s hard-timeout safety ceiling. Result dict `_result` shared between threads (worker writes before `_done.set()`; reader checks after `_done.wait()` returns — no race).
- **Swimming shark** (`gui/animations.py:SharkSwimIndicator`) — now paints the 🦈 in `paintEvent` with vertical bob, tail-flick rotation, direction-facing flip, and a bubble wake (was a flat slide). Shown next to INITIATE SHARK ATTACK during a drill.
- **Posture Hardening** (`modules/posture_hardening.py`, auto-registered) — self-healing loop: tails the after-action report; any SUCCESS / LOW-DETECTION technique → row in `agent_memory.db:system_weaknesses` (VULNERABLE), drops module health <50, and stages a deterministic (temp 0) Ollama-generated PowerShell/registry remediation to `remediations/<mitre>.ps1`. REVIEW-GATED: `execute_remediation(mitre, authorized=True)` and `execute_custom_patch(raw, mode)` — mode `AI-Assisted` cleans+stages via Ollama (no run); `Direct Native` runs the user's OWN authorized script (`powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass`), captures stdout/stderr, logs it. Never auto-executes.
- **World View** (`telemetry/worldview.py:WorldViewEngine` + `gui/worldview_page.py:WorldViewDialog`, header 🌐 button) — (1) host↔suite resource matrix (psutil RSS/CPU/threads vs host), (2) EPS blinding detector (internal EventBus EPS vs host ctx-switch rate → "[CRITICAL: Telemetry Mismatch / Blinding Attack Detected]", gated on a proven feed), (3) Ollama diagnostics (VRAM, tokens/sec, queue via /api/ps + probe).
- **Launcher** (`create-launcher.ps1`) — creates a desktop "Angerona.lnk" → `start-angerona.bat` with the app icon; attempts taskbar pin.

## LATEST (v1.6.1 — remediation-routing fix, selfcheck grid assertions, cold-start self_test parity, syntax warnings)
- **Fixed:** `modules/remediation_actions.py` — T1562/AMSI-bypass alerts were shadowed by `RegistryHardeningAction`'s bare `"t1562"` substring match (that MITRE ID also covers script-block-logging defense evasion) and never reached `DefenderHardeningAction`. `RegistryHardeningAction._matches()` now requires the specific `"t1562.011"` sub-technique (or explicit script-block/logging keywords) so a bare T1562/AMSI-bypass alert routes to Defender hardening instead.
- **Fixed:** two stale assertions in `tools/selfcheck.py` still checked the old 7-node single-row World View flow; updated to the current 6-node 2×3 grid (`{"capture","detect","triage","respond","attack","harden"}`), matching `gui/flow_window.py`/`core/flow_metrics.py`.
- **Fixed:** cold-start `self_test()` inconsistency across 6 modules (AMSI Bridge, Dynamic Resource Governor, Memory Injection Scanner, Sysmon Event Bridge, TUNE, WFP Controller) — each now falls back to `super().self_test()` when not yet started, reporting the same graceful "stopped" status as the other ~50 modules instead of a spurious failure.
- **Fixed:** two `SyntaxWarning`s from unescaped backslash sequences in string literals — `posture_hardening.py:615` and `ransomware_heuristics.py:25`.
- Verified: `tools/selfcheck.py` — 26/26 PASS.

## LATEST (v1.1.0 — hardening, reliability, AI firewall)
- **Fixed:** `modules/api_patch_detector.py` PE parser used +96 (PE32) for PE32+ too → parsed 0 ntdll exports on 64-bit (APID was dead). Now +112 for PE32+.
- **Self-hardening:** `core/hardening.py` — SetProcessMitigationPolicy on our own process at startup (ExtensionPointDisable, ImageLoad no-remote/no-low-IL, ASLR; ACG opt-in via `ANGERONA_HARDEN_AGGRESSIVE=1`). MicrosoftSignedOnly NEVER applied (blocks Qt DLLs).
- **Judgment Gate:** `posture_hardening.py` SHA-256-stamps staged scripts; `execute_remediation` re-hashes and BLOCKS tampered scripts. AI decisions → `diagnostics/remediation_attempts.log`.
- **Vetted active remediation:** `modules/remediation_actions.py` — AI *selects* from a fixed allow-list (never authors runnable code): quarantine, disable-driver-service, registry hardening (LSA RunAsPPL / WDigest UseLogonCredential), ACL lockdown (icacls save+restore), Defender baseline re-assert. All reversible where possible; each verifies then rolls back on failure. Host-level actions are gated OFF unless `ANGERONA_AUTO_REMEDIATE=1`. `posture_hardening.apply_vetted_remediation(apply=)` dry-runs by default.
- **Network isolation (vetted action):** `remediation_actions.NetworkIsolationAction` — reversible host-firewall block of a C2/exfil peer IP (`netsh advfirewall` named rule, deleted on rollback). `_first_ip_in()` ignores loopback/link-local. Host-level → gated by `ANGERONA_AUTO_REMEDIATE=1`. Cross-checked by the ATT&CK map so coverage can't claim a response we don't ship.
- **Persistence Sweep (`modules/persistence_sweep.py`):** baselines + monitors autorun surfaces — Run/RunOnce + Winlogon Shell/Userinit (winreg), startup folders, services (psutil), scheduled tasks (schtasks) + WMI event consumers (PowerShell CIM, slower cadence). Pure `_classify()` escalates: encoded-PS/LOLBin + Winlogon hijack → CRITICAL, user-writable path → HIGH. Read-only enumeration; subprocess surfaces polled every 5th cycle to stay light. Maps T1547/T1053/T1543/T1546.
- **ATT&CK coverage (`core/attack_coverage.py`):** curated technique→(Detect/Simulate/Remediate) map; Remediate column cross-checked at runtime vs the real `ACTIONS` allow-list (no phantom coverage). `render()` = text heatmap; console `coverage`/`attack`/`mitre`. Currently 15/18 techniques covered.
- **Incident correlation (`core/incidents.py`):** O(1)/event bus subscriber (wired in `app.py`) that rolls related alerts into time-windowed, risk-scored incidents (non-linear severity weights + breadth bonus; 120s window closes an incident). Ignores INFO/Console noise, bounded memory. Console: `incidents [n]`, `incident <id>`.
- **Remediation log (`core/remediation_log.py`):** persistent SQLite audit ledger for every remediation action — applied, skipped, dry-run, rolled-back, or errored. Stored in `remediation_log` table in the same `flight-recorder.db` as FlightRecorder. Singleton `get_log()` / `init_log(db_path)` wired in `app.py`. `apply_remediation()` in `remediation_actions.py` writes one row per action slot (accepts `trigger` + `db_path` kwargs). `posture_hardening.apply_vetted_remediation()` passes `trigger="PostureHardening"`. Console: `remlog [n]` (newest-first table with outcome icon, MITRE, action, verify flag, caller); `remlog <T####>` (filter by technique).
- **Ring 1 Driver Shield:** `intel_sync.is_known_bad_driver()` (offline BYOVD blocklist) + `file_integrity.py` watches `System32\drivers`, flags `.sys`/known-bad writes CRITICAL. Shark has a benign BYOVD marker technique.
- **Jitter:** `core/jitter.jittered()` (`os.urandom`) on DRILL canary + FRZ heartbeat. Ollama `keep_alive` on hot paths.
- **SOAR:** self-kill guard (never kills our own PID); drill lowers threshold so it remediates benign markers (AAR shows remediation).
- **Forensics UI (`gui/pages.py`):** clickable stat cards → detail windows; `build_blast_tree(pid)` PROV tree; Shark-vs-Shield CollisionView; dashboard FORENSICS menu.
- **Unified sim:** one red RUN RED TEAM SIMULATION button + `RedTeamSimulationDialog` (scenarios, difficulty=recursive phases, target dir, persistent custom-technique library w/ CRUD). Custom text is an INERT marker, NEVER executed. Shark/sword animations removed (`main_window._NoAnim`).
- **Reliability:** self-test runs off-thread, prompts to fix on failure, writes `diagnostics/selftest_failures.json`; `core/uiwatchdog.py` dumps thread stacks to `diagnostics/not_responding.log` on a GUI stall; console spinner is `WORKING…` (bigger). Two-pane Live Offense Monitor (offense | Flight Instructor).
- **Analyst console (`core/commands.py`):** + netstat, contain/isolate, sessions/whoami, timeline, iocs, search/grep, hashes/sha256, uptime, env, report, incidents, incident <id>, coverage/attack/mitre, remlog/actions (remediation audit log).
- **AI firewall (`engines/ai_guardrail.py`):** optional FastAPI proxy on :8000 in front of Ollama — injection heuristics, DoS/token cap, immutable hardened system-prompt wrapper, PII/secret/path output redaction, JSON audit → `diagnostics/ai_security_audit.log`. Pure logic tested; FastAPI/uvicorn lazy-imported. Run: `python -m angerona.engines.ai_guardrail`.
- **HW patch (`core/hw_profile.py`):** pynvml VRAM profile → tiered config (6 GB/GTX 1060 → gemma:2b, batch 4096, ctx 4096, `free_gpu_pools()`; ≥12 GB → llama3:8b). CPU-tier fallback.
- **Sprint-1 threat-model fixes (BL-02/03/08):** guardrail per-session token (`ai_guardrail.check_token` / `SESSION_TOKEN`); single guarded client `engines/ollama_client.py` (route ALL model calls through it); `neutralize_telemetry()` delimits/defuses attacker-influenced telemetry before the model (wired into `ai_triage`); Judgment-Gate TOCTOU CLOSED — `execute_remediation` reads-once, verifies that read, runs the exact bytes from a locked temp copy. Remaining call sites should adopt `ollama_client`.

## LATEST (v1.3.0 cont. 5 — BlackBox UX: firewall detail panel, sortable tables, red highlights, auto-inspect)
- **Firewall rule detail on click (`blackbox_recorder.py:FirewallRuleDetailDialog`)** — clicking any row in the "8 · Firewall Rules" tab opens a `FirewallRuleDetailDialog(QDialog)`. Known fields (Direction/Action/Enabled) are shown immediately; a `_FwDetailWorker(QThread)` then runs `Get-NetFirewallRule -Name / Get-NetFirewallApplicationFilter / PortFilter / AddressFilter` in the background and populates Description, Profile, Program Path, Protocol, Local/Remote Port, Local/Remote Address. **📋 Copy File Path** copies the program path to the clipboard; **📂 Open File Location** opens Explorer at its parent directory. Both buttons are disabled (greyed) when the rule has no specific program path ("Any"). PS primary path stores the internal `Name` field as `rule_id`; netsh fallback stores `DisplayName` + `use_display=True` so the detail query falls back to `-DisplayName`. Single-instance guard in `_on_rule_clicked` resolves the clicked row back by display name after sort.
- **Sortable columns on all 8 tabs** — every `QTableWidget` across all tabs now has `setSortingEnabled(True)`; populate methods wrap inserts with `setSortingEnabled(False/True)` to prevent mid-fill sort thrashing. Column-header click sorts ascending/descending.
- **Amber highlights → Red** — all `QColor(AMBER)` foreground highlights replaced with `QColor(RED)`: crash-inspector hint lines, blocked/disk-sleep threads (ThreadTab), recently-changed config drift files, locked DB/mmap handles (ConfigTab), Inbound firewall direction (FirewallTab), HIGH severity SOAR events (SoarEventsTab). FROZEN state colour in HealthTab state badge retained as amber (semantically different from a hard failure).
- **CrashInspector auto-inspect on paste** — `QLineEdit.textChanged` now connected to `_on_paste_changed()`; as soon as pasted text matches `_TRACE_LINE` (`File "...", line N`) the inspector auto-opens the file and scrolls to the failing line in red — no button click required.
- **BlackBox startup crash log** — `main()` wrapped in try/except that writes `diagnostics/blackbox_startup.log` so silent `pythonw` failures are diagnosable.
- **`start-angerona.bat` — added `--show`** to the BlackBox launch so the window appears immediately; previously launched without `--show` which minimised to taskbar and was then blocked by app.py's single-instance guard before `--show` could run.
- **`QTextEdit` missing import fix** — `QTextEdit` was used by `CrashInspector.code` but absent from the `PySide6.QtWidgets` import block; added alongside `QDialog` and `QFormLayout` (needed by new detail dialog).

## LATEST (v1.3.0 cont. 4 — startup crash fixes)
- **`QAction` import fix (`gui/pages.py`)** — `QAction` was listed under `from PySide6.QtWidgets import (...)` but in PySide6 6.x it lives in `PySide6.QtGui`. Moved to the `QtGui` line alongside `QColor`, `QFont`, etc. This was the sole cause of the `ImportError: cannot import name 'QAction' from 'PySide6.QtWidgets'` startup crash. Stale `pages.cpython-314.pyc` deleted so Python 3.14 recompiles clean.
- **BlackBox `on_launch_sandbox` method (`blackbox_recorder.py:BlackBoxWindow`)** — `__init__` connected `self.btn_sandbox.clicked.connect(self.on_launch_sandbox)` but the method was never defined; PySide6 evaluates the attribute immediately in `connect()`, raising `AttributeError` before the window could show. Added `on_launch_sandbox()` (`@Slot()`) that spawns `angerona.gui.sandbox_editor` as a detached subprocess via `Popen([sys.executable, "-m", "angerona.gui.sandbox_editor"], cwd=APP_DIR, creationflags=CREATE_NO_WINDOW)`, matching the pattern used by the main app's `_launch_blackbox()`. Stale `blackbox_recorder.cpython-314.pyc` deleted to force recompile.

## LATEST (v1.3.0 cont. 3 — SOAR AI per-alert consult, ModuleInspector tabs, BlackBox expansion, MINJ enrichment)
- **SOAR panel AI consultation (`gui/pages.py:SoarPanel`)** — `🤖 Ask AI (selected)` toolbar button sends ALL queued alerts to AI in one structured prompt; right-click any SOAR queue row → `🤖 Consult AI on this alert` opens a dedicated `AIConsultDialog` per alert. `_gather_system_context()` snapshot (OS/host, RAM%, CPU%, proc count, conn count, uptime, VPN tunnel interfaces, unhealthy modules, recent CRITICAL events from the bus) is prepended to every prompt. Enhanced `_enrich()` gathers: psutil process name+parent, all TCP connections for the PID, SHA-256 of the file (files <64 MB), Windows Authenticode signature via PowerShell `Get-AuthenticodeSignature`, file size/ctime/mtime, and VPN classification via `core/net_interfaces`. Non-modal dialogs tracked via `_show_nonmodal()` + `_OPEN_DIALOGS` registry to prevent duplicates.
- **Settings API Keys — AI provider order (`gui/pages.py:SettingsDialog._tab_apikeys`)** — `QListWidget` shows the 5 providers (Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Ollama) with ▲/▼ reorder buttons; order is persisted to `Config.ai_provider_order` (→ `settings.json`) and published to `ANGERONA_AI_ORDER` env var on save. `engines/ai_consult._ordered_providers()` reads this env var so runtime provider selection matches the operator's preference. `SettingsDialog._save()` (was broken/missing) is now fully implemented: saves ollama URL, theme, autostart enable/disable, eco, blackbox, MCP, eBPF, mobile signal-cli settings, AI provider order → `Config.save()`.
- **ModuleInspector tabs (`gui/pages.py:ModuleInspector`)** — three new tabs wired into the 2 s refresh timer: **Performance** (scan interval/_throttle, event rates 5 min/60 min, health %, thread status, sparkbar trend up to 60 samples); **History** (full EventBus log for this module with keyword filter, click → `AlertDetailDialog`); **Dependencies** (source file path via `inspect.getfile` + rglob fallback, AST-parsed import list marking `[internal]` for `angerona.*` imports, module metadata: version, category, enabled_by_default). New helpers: `_find_module_src()`, `_parse_imports()`, `_refresh_history()`.
- **BlackBox recorder expansion (`blackbox_recorder.py`)** — two new tabs: **7 · SOAR Events** (`SoarEventsTab`) reads `soar_queue.json` from both APP_DIR and DATA_DIR, deduplicates by (ts, message[:60]), RED=CRITICAL / AMBER=HIGH; **8 · Firewall Rules** (`FirewallTab`) runs PowerShell `Get-NetFirewallRule | ConvertTo-Json` primary, `netsh advfirewall firewall show rule name=all` fallback, keyword filter, AMBER=Inbound / RED=Block / GREEN=Allow. Both included in "Generate Diagnostic Bundle .zip" as `soar_events.txt` + `firewall_rules.txt`.
- **Memory Injection Scanner enrichment (`modules/mem_inject_scanner.py`, MINJ)** — `_enrich_process(pid)` collects: exe path, cmdline (first 8 tokens), username, status, thread count, process age (h/m/s), parent name+PID, child count, loaded DLL count, active TCP connections (up to 8), RSS KB + VMS MB. `_predict_technique(proc_name, size, protect, ctx)` classifies likely technique (high-value system process / C2 carrier / process hollowing / packed binary / reflective DLL / shellcode stub) and builds MITRE sub-technique list (T1055 base + T1055.001, T1055.012, T1071, T1078.003 conditionally). Alert messages are now multi-line with all enriched fields; `predicted_technique` key in `details`.

## LATEST (v1.3.0 — Eco stampede fix, Live-Fire Sandbox, online-AI consult, resource strip)
- **Eco Mode auto-on + sequential wake-up** — `Config.eco_mode` (default True, Settings ▸ Performance toggle) starts the app with heavy scanners paused for a fast, responsive launch; `app._load_modules` fires `MainWindow.startup_eco_requested` (queued signal) after `start_enabled()`. Turning Eco OFF now uses **`core/eco_wakeup.py:EcoWakeupWorker`** (QThread): wakes paused modules ONE AT A TIME with a per-module health-gate (`health>0`+running, else `status=="error"`/2.5 s timeout), signals `module_waking`/`module_ready`/`wakeup_complete`. Fixes the "memory stampede" that froze the UI when ~19 threads rescanned at once.
- **Live-Fire Sandbox & Editor (`gui/sandbox_editor.py`, SBOX)** — standalone PySide6 module isolation + hot-swap editor. On launch: `manager.stop_all()`, mutes the EventBus (swaps `bus.publish` for a no-op, restored on close), and overrides the brand banner to "⚠️ DIAGNOSTIC OVERRIDE: TESTING IN PROGRESS". Left module tree · center `QPlainTextEdit` with a `PythonHighlighter` · right test/history console. Buttons: Open · Run Isolated Test (`self_test()` on a decoupled thread, stdout/stderr captured) · Apply Changes (QMessageBox warning → **`ast.parse()` gate** → write → history → `importlib.reload()` + rebind + restart) · Revert to Previous (in-mem + temp backups) · View History. Reload/restart failures are pushed onto the live alert feed. Launch from the FORENSICS menu ("Live-Fire Sandbox & Editor…"), a module window's "✎ Edit code (Sandbox)" button, or `python -m angerona.gui.sandbox_editor`.
- **Online AI consult (`engines/ai_consult.py` + `gui/ai_consult_dialog.py`)** — user-initiated (= consented) online consult, **Anthropic/Claude first**, then OpenAI → OpenRouter → Gemini → local Ollama fallback (stdlib urllib; keys from Settings ▸ API Keys / `.env`). `AIConsultDialog` runs it off-thread and offers **Save to computer / Discard**. Surfaced as: Threat-Intel dashboard "🌐 Consult AI"; CVE Deep-Analysis window "🌐 Consult AI" (fallback-OK) + "💡 AI Proposed Solution" (online-only) that build a full CVE fix/patch file; and alert-detail "🔎 Research".
- **Alert action buttons everywhere (`gui/pages.py`)** — `AlertDetailDialog` now carries **Allow · Block · Analyze · Research** (Block/Analyze work standalone via the bus/`AnalysisWorker`, or reuse the AlertsPanel when opened from it). A module window's recent-events feed is now clickable and opens the SAME detail dialog, so module alerts get the same actions as the main Live Alerts feed.
- **Bottom bars upgraded (`gui/pages.py`)** — StatusStrip chips are clickable (`_ClickableChip`) → open that module's full window (`ModuleInspector`: details, live alerts, self-test, restart, Edit-code). New **`ResourceStrip`** second row shows per-module resource-intensity 0–100% (0=stopped→red, low→green, busy→amber→orange-red; heuristic = heaviness weight + live event rate). `ModulesPanel` gains a **Sort by: Name / On-Off / Status / Category** selector.
- **Console commands (`core/commands.py`)** — `intel`/`kev`/`fetchintel` (summarise host-applicable CISA KEV from `shared_logs/upstream_threats.json`), `consult <q>` (online AI, Claude-first), `resources`/`resmon`/`load` (per-module intensity snapshot).
- **Deception hygiene** — canaries + honeytokens are now written **HIDDEN|SYSTEM** (`deception.py`, `smart_deception.py`) so they never clutter Desktop/Documents. **Red-team auto-cleanup** (`shark/red_team.py`): every marker is `_redteam_*` + hidden, swept on start (orphans from crashed runs), on `stop_and_clean()`, and ~55 s after a drill ends (post-detection settle) — the drill no longer litters Documents.
- **Tooling** — `tools/compile_check.py` + `run-compile-check.bat`: fast stdlib syntax gate over `src/angerona` (run before `run-selfcheck.bat`).
- **Fix — Settings button (defensive):** `_open_settings` is now wrapped so a construction error surfaces in a dialog + console instead of silently doing nothing (the classic "button does nothing"). (Prior root cause — `apply_theme` signature — remains fixed.)
- **Situational awareness — Threat Posture score (`core/posture.py`)** — composite 0–100 indicator under the brand (band + colour: Secure/Guarded/Elevated/High/Critical), updated each tick from active HIGH/CRITICAL detections + degraded/stopped modules + host-applicable KEV count + ATT&CK heat. Click it for a factor breakdown. Never raises (missing signal = 0 contribution).
- **Situational awareness — CRITICAL tray notifications (`main_window._notify_critical`)** — new CRITICAL bus detections raise a Windows tray/toast (`QSystemTrayIcon.showMessage`, 8 s throttle) so the operator stays aware while minimized.
- **Situational awareness — Top Talkers (`gui/top_talkers.py`)** — live "who is my machine talking to": established outbound connections grouped by process, untrusted-external count flagged red, interface type, optional reverse-DNS, 4 s refresh. Opens from the FORENSICS menu ("Top Talkers — outbound network…"). psutil walk + `net_interfaces` enrichment.
- **Research follow-up chat (`gui/ai_consult_dialog.py`)** — the AI consult/Research window gains a "💬 Ask AI" button that reveals a prompt bar for a multi-turn conversation (context carried across turns) alongside Save/Discard.
- **Fix — splitter drag** — the Modules|Alerts and body|Console `QSplitter`s now use non-opaque resize + `setChildrenCollapsible(False)` + a 7 px handle + per-panel minimum sizes, fixing the glitchy/unreliable panel-resize drag (heavy tables were thrashing under opaque resize).
- **Adaptive Resource Governor (`modules/resource_governor.py`, GOV)** — samples this process's CPU every 5 s and, under sustained load (>22%/core), raises `BaseModule._throttle` on heavy **non-critical** running modules so their `self.sleep()` loops run up to 6× slower (lower CPU); relaxes back to 1× when load drops (<10%/core). Works in BOTH Eco and normal mode. NEVER throttles the real-time protection path (SOAR, Watchdog, Heartbeat, IPC Guard, AI Triage, Active Deception, Ransomware Heuristics, AMSI, Fast-Path, Shadow Shield). Cooperative only — never suspends/kills threads; fail-open.
- **`BaseModule` throttle hook** — new `self._throttle` (default 1.0) + `set_throttle()`; `sleep()` multiplies its wait by it, so any module using `self.sleep()` for cadence is governable for free.
- **Baseline perf** — Shadow Shield's Documents/Desktop stat-walk moved 5 s → 15 s (~3× less steady CPU; VSS + RANS remain the real ransomware net); Smart Deception decoy-check 1 s → 2.5 s. Posture score caches its KEV file read (mtime + 30 s TTL) and, with the ResourceStrip, now refreshes every ~4 s instead of every tick — removing a per-second disk read/compute the first v1.3.0 cut introduced.
- **Black Box auto-launch (`blackbox_recorder.py` at repo root)** — the decoupled, strictly READ-ONLY out-of-band diagnostic recorder (own process, tray-resident, shares nothing with the suite so it survives a suite deadlock; 6 tabs: live crash/error tail, host telemetry graphs, suite-health/bus-liveness, thread-state, memory profiler, config-drift; "Generate Diagnostic Bundle .zip"). It was never being started — now `app._launch_blackbox()` spawns it ~800 ms after the window shows with `--show`, detached + windowless (`pythonw`/`CREATE_NO_WINDOW`), single-instance guarded, fail-open, and **captures the recorder's own stdout/stderr to `<data_dir>/logs/blackbox.log`** with a 3.5 s liveness check that reports to the console if it died. Gated by `Config.blackbox_enabled` (default True; Settings ▸ Performance ▸ Black Box toggle). Covered by the F: backup and tracked in git.
- **Black Box resilience + reach** — QtCharts (PySide6-Addons) import is now **optional** (missing Addons no longer crashes the recorder on launch — the telemetry tab drops to numeric bars). The recorder now watches BOTH the repo `diagnostics/` AND the per-user **data dir** (`ANGERONA_DATA`/`%LOCALAPPDATA%\Angerona`) — so it sees `crash_snapshots/`, the real `flight-recorder.db`, `logs/crash.log`, and `status.json` that live there. It also tails `diagnostics/runtime_alerts.log`.
- **Crash/stall evidence → Black Box** — `core/crashlog.py` (already installed in `__main__`) mirrors unhandled exceptions / faulthandler native faults / Qt-fatal to `<data_dir>/logs/crash.log` + `diagnostics/crash.log`; `uiwatchdog` writes `diagnostics/not_responding.log` on a GUI stall. NEW: `MainWindow._blackbox_feed()` appends every CRITICAL notification (and non-fatal UI-refresh errors) to `diagnostics/runtime_alerts.log`, which the Black Box tails — so "went non-responsive / got a CRITICAL" always reaches the recorder.
- **Anti-crash under load** — `MainWindow._refresh` is fully wrapped (per-panel guarded) so a panel raising under a data flood can't propagate out of the QTimer and kill the app; worst case one repaint tick is skipped. EventBus already rings at 500 with INFO backpressure.
- **Governor is now memory-aware + more aggressive** — thresholds CPU>18%/core or RAM≥85% throttle a step; severe (CPU>36%/core or RAM≥92%) jumps 2 steps; max slowdown 8× (was 6×); sample 4 s. This sheds load before the machine thrashes/OOMs (the actual cause of the random crashes).
- **Black Box desktop launcher + icon** — `assets/icons/blackbox.ico` (black box on a blue background) + `create-blackbox-launcher.ps1` creates a Desktop "Angerona Black Box" shortcut (venv `pythonw … --show`, that icon) and an "Angerona" shortcut if one doesn't exist. The recorder's own window/tray icon now loads that `.ico`.

## LATEST (v1.3.0 cont. — mobile remote control, Linux eBPF, SGX confidential compute)
- **Mobile Response Bridge (`modules/mobile_bridge.py`, MOB_BRDG)** — E2EE remote orchestration over Signal (`signal-cli`). Opt-in (`Config.mobile_enabled`, Settings ▸ Mobile Integration; fields locked until enabled; 4-digit **PIN is DPAPI-wrapped** into `.env` as `ANGERONA_MOBILE_PIN_DPAPI` via `hardware_crypto.protect`). Thread always runs but self-gates (idles when disabled/unconfigured). HIGH/CRITICAL bus alerts → `neutralize_telemetry()` → random single-use 4-digit token in `self.pending_alerts`; TTL sweep every 10 s prunes tokens >10 min and auto-SUSPENDs + notifies; >3 alerts/60 s aggregate into one digest text. Command parser (from the operator number only; unknown/failed input silently discarded + logged as "Spoof/Unauthorized Access Attempt" with msg SHA): **HELP** (verbatim menu, bypasses tokens), **STATUS** (posture+KEV), **DIAG** (proc CPU/RAM/threads), **ECO ON/OFF** (drives the Governor throttle), **LOCKDOWN <PIN>** (host isolation directive), **KILL/SUSPEND <TOKEN> <PIN>** (signed mitigation directive on the bus, token erased), **ROLLBACK <TOKEN> <PIN>** (Shadow Shield `trigger_rollback`), **MUTE <TOKEN>** (15-min per-module suppression). signal-cli runs as short subprocesses off the UI thread.
- **Linux eBPF sensor (`modules/ebpf_sensor.py`, EBPF)** — headless-Linux node sensor using BCC: inline eBPF C hooks `execve` (PID/UID/argv0) + `tcp_sendmsg` (PID/dst/port) with `BPF_PERF_OUTPUT`, kernel-side noise drop; `perf_buffer_poll` → Angerona `Event`s emitted on the bus (RBRG forwards to the Windows GUI). Opt-in (`Config.ebpf_enabled`, Settings ▸ System). Frees BPF maps on stop; wraps BCC init in try/except → health 10 % + graceful idle without root / headers / BCC; healthy-inert on Windows.
- **Confidential Compute — Intel SGX / Gramine (`core/sgx_guard.py` + `angerona.manifest.template`)** — `is_confidential_compute_active()` (checks `/dev/attestation/*` + Gramine env) for app.py / the posture dashboard; `SgxEnclaveGuard` generates an AES-256 key strictly in enclave memory and best-effort patches the MEMC `:memory:` SQLite to SQLCipher (`PRAGMA key`), no-op outside an enclave. The manifest graminizes the Python runtime (2 G enclave, 128 threads), maps `/src` read-only + an encrypted tmpfs, seals the IPC key to MRENCLAVE, allows the live `flight-recorder.db`, and keeps loopback (`:47921`) intact.

## LATEST (v1.2.0 — behavioural tuning, VPN awareness, hardware integrity, multi-node)
- **TUNE (`modules/behavioral_tuner.py`)** — Learning Engine + Safe-Path Interceptor (inverse of FPTH). Silent-audit window (default 7d) builds a 3-way `behavioral_baseline` (SQLite): SHA-256 hash + parent→child lineage + network /24-subnet+port. `check_event()`/`is_known_good()` downgrade a perfect match to INFO so backpressure drops it *before* Ollama; drift handler refreshes a hash silently on benign auto-update. `export_wfp_rules()` / `export_kernel_rules()` emit Ring-0 offload payloads (WFPC / AngeronaSensor.sys). Module-global `get_tuner()` lets `ai_triage` skip inference on baselined events (fail-open).
- **HEAL (`modules/self_healer.py`)** — Self-Debugging Co-Pilot (Try→Heal→Stage). Tails `diagnostics/crash_snapshots/`, packs traceback+source into a constrained Ollama prompt, `ast`-validates the returned code, and STAGES it to `staged_patches/<MOD>_fix_v<N>.py` (never overwrites live) + HIGH alert. GUI "Apply Patch" is the human gate.
- **SHDW (`modules/shadow_shield.py`)** — ransomware file shielding: delta version cache (`trigger_rollback()` restores pre-encryption copies) + quiet VSS snapshots (WMI/`vssadmin`). NEVER deletes shadow copies.
- **SDEC (`modules/smart_deception.py`)** — hyper-contextual honeytokens: samples Documents *names* only → local Ollama invents blended decoy names → drops into Desktop/Documents/%APPDATA%; any touch ⇒ CRITICAL. Static fallback list if Ollama down.
- **CHAOS (`modules/chaos_harness.py`)** — security chaos engineering "bug killer": on a slow cycle fires SAFE probes (APID cooperative drill signal — never a real hook; high-entropy DNS → NDRD; EICAR write → FIM/AMSI) and verifies the detector echoes on the bus within a timeout; missing echo ⇒ blind-sensor alert.
- **RBRG (`modules/remote_bridge.py`)** — secure multi-node telemetry forwarding. SENDER polls the bus and forwards HIGH/CRITICAL to the main PC; RECEIVER binds a LAN port, authenticates via shared-key HMAC challenge/response (extends AUTH), and republishes with `node_origin`/`hostname`. OFF by default (`enabled_by_default=False`); refuses to open the network without `ANGERONA_BRIDGE_MODE` + peer/port + `ANGERONA_BRIDGE_KEY`/`bridge.key`. Drops+logs unauthenticated peers.
- **HWID (`modules/hardware_crypto.py`)** — hardware-rooted integrity. DPAPI-wraps the AUTH IPC key to a user/host-bound sidecar `ipc_auth.key.dpapi` (win32crypt → ctypes fallback; verifies round-trip; live key untouched). `bind_db_key_to_tpm()` outlines TPM 2.0 sealing of the DB key via tpm2-pytss. Inert (no crash) off-Windows.
- **Headless mode (`core/headless.py`, `__main__.py --headless`)** — builds Config/EventBus/FlightRecorder/incidents/attack_tracker/remediation_log/ModuleManager WITHOUT importing PySide6; starts enabled modules; blocks until SIGINT/SIGTERM. For silent sensor / home-server nodes (pair with RBRG SENDER).
- **VPN awareness (`core/net_interfaces.py`)** — psutil-only interface classifier (Physical/Virtual_VPN/Loopback via name/OUI/point-to-point heuristics, 30 s cache). `network_monitor` stamps `interface_type` on NET events; `ai_triage` passes it into the Ollama prompt; **FPTH rule #18 (split-tunnel abuse)** — `detect_split_tunnel(pid)` fires CRITICAL when one PID holds concurrent VPN + physical-external connections.
- **GUI (`gui/pages.py`, `core/analysis_worker.py`)** — Alerts panel gains a 7th **Analyze** column: `AnalysisWorker` (QThread) runs dual-stage deep triage (local llama3:8b → cloud CTI if confidence <70 or UNKNOWN) off the GUI thread; button disables to "Analyzing…" so the GPU queue can't be spammed. `MarqueeLabel` slowly scrolls long alert text in the detail dialog. Remote alerts show a `⇠node_origin` tag.
- **Fix — Settings button:** `MainWindow.apply_theme()` now takes an optional `theme` arg; `SettingsDialog._save` passed one, so any theme-change Save previously raised `TypeError` and the dialog never closed/applied. Settings now work.

## NEVER DO
- NEVER edit the `D:\local-security-ai` root terminal prototype for GUI changes — wrong app.
- NEVER block the Qt main thread; module work runs in BaseModule daemon threads; GUI updates via signals/QTimer.
- NEVER auto-execute a remediation or custom patch — always review-gated; loopback (Ollama :11434, IPC) stays reachable.
- NEVER add a second app instance (singleton :47921) or bypass the STOP/kill-all hard shutdown.
- NEVER send host data off-machine without consent; Ollama local-first, cloud only on threshold via `cloud_fallback`.
- NEVER enable Remote Bridge (RBRG) implicitly — it is the ONLY off-box egress path. It stays `enabled_by_default=False` and must not open a socket without an explicit shared key + peer. HWID DPAPI-wraps the IPC key but must NEVER mutate the live `ipc_auth.key` AUTH reads (protected sidecar only).
